chunk_summary: Guidelines for screening and follow-up intervals in obese adolescents, emphasizing comorbidity evaluation, lifestyle interventions, and specialist care for severe cases.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of standardized comorbidity screening in obese adolescents.
Learn the recommended follow-up intervals and lifestyle interventions for managing adolescent obesity.
Identify key risk factors such as hypertension, dyslipidemia, and PCOS in obese adolescents.
Recognize when to escalate care to pharmacotherapy or bariatric surgery.
key_facts: Use IAP 2015 charts or WHO 5–19 references for adolescent obesity definitions.
Screen obese adolescents ≥10 years for hypertension, dyslipidemia, hyperglycemia, and fatty liver risk.
Follow-up every 4–6 weeks initially, then every 3 months once stable, with annual lab reassessment.
Include fasting lipid profile, fasting glucose or HbA1c, and ALT in comorbidity screening.
PCOS evaluation in adolescent girls should consider clinical/biochemical hyperandrogenism; ultrasound alone is unreliable in early puberty.
Pharmacotherapy and bariatric surgery are reserved for severe cases after lifestyle intervention failure.
reference_quote: IAP obesity guidance recommends that all obese adolescents ≥10 years are evaluated for hypertension, dyslipidemia, hyperglycemia, and fatty liver risk.
document_key_topics: Follow-up intervals for adolescent obesity management
Specialist care for severe obesity cases
